cobalt-signal.$description: Cobalt Signal — Violet action color for filled buttons, selected navigation states, and focused conversion moments

ember-vermillion.$description: Ember Vermillion — Brand mark accent, the Jasper wordmark stem, and the rare warm fill. Used sparingly so it never dilutes the cobalt's primacy as the action color

midnight-ink.$description: Midnight Ink — Deep navy-violet for icon fills and emphasis body copy. Sits between black and cobalt in the chromatic hierarchy — a quieter alternative to pure black when an element needs a hint of brand color

periwinkle-mist.$description: Periwinkle Mist — Soft lavender-blue used as a ghost/secondary button surface. Reads as a desaturated echo of Cobalt Signal — the same hue at low saturation, signaling a non-primary action without going neutral

bone.$description: Bone — Page canvas, hairline borders, and the dominant surface tone. With 1000+ occurrences it is the structural backbone of the system — everything else sits on it or is carved out of it

ink-black.$description: Ink Black — Maximum-emphasis headings, primary text, and icon strokes. Reserved for the loudest moments; never used for body copy where 2d2d/404040 take over

charcoal.$description: Charcoal — Headings and button text — slightly softer than pure black for elements that need weight but not the harshest contrast

graphite.$description: Graphite — Body copy, secondary headings, and icon fills. The workhorse neutral — used wherever readability matters but Ink Black feels too heavy

slate.$description: Slate — Muted helper text, captions, and low-emphasis labels. The lightest text color that still passes on bone canvas

frost.$description: Frost — Subtle dividers and elevated surface tone, one step darker than the canvas bone. Used when a section needs a quiet visual break

ash.$description: Ash — Card and tag backgrounds in compact density zones. Mid-gray surface that lifts content off the bone canvas without introducing color

onyx.$description: Onyx — Deep alternative to Ink Black for links and emphasis icons when the element needs to feel slightly warmer or paired with cobalt

paper-white.$description: Paper White — Button text on cobalt fills, and the second surface tier for cards/elevated content layered over bone canvas

primary.$description: Primary — detected in extracted data but not described by AI

custom-sans-likely-inter-or-a-geometric-.$value: Custom Sans (likely Inter or a geometric humanist substitute)
custom-sans-likely-inter-or-a-geometric-.$description: Body copy, UI labels, button text, captions, card titles. The utilitarian workhorse — never decorative. Weight 500 for emphasis, 600 for card titles, 400 for body and labels.

editorial-serif-custom-high-contrast-mod.$value: Editorial Serif (custom — high-contrast modern serif, Didone family)
editorial-serif-custom-high-contrast-mod.$description: Hero and section headlines only. The signature choice — a whisper-weight serif at display sizes against an otherwise sans-serif interface creates editorial tension. Weight 400 at 96px is anti-convention; most AI tool brands use 700 sans-serif, this trusts the typeface's contrast and tracking to carry authority.

secondaryfont.$description: SecondaryFont — detected in extracted data but not described by AI

canvas.$description: Surface level 1: Full-page background — the bone paper that the entire system is printed on.

card-surface.$description: Surface level 2: Tool cards, FAQ items, and any content block that needs to lift off the canvas.

subtle-elevation.$description: Surface level 3: Quiet section dividers and inline emphasis blocks, one shade darker than canvas.
